prompt: Create a .NET 9 Order aggregate root with line items, value objects (Money, Quantity), Result<T>, and business rules (min 1 item, positive qty, non-negative price, $10K max, no adds after confirm). Include xUnit tests.
assertions: Value objects (Money, Quantity) are separate classes with value equality, not raw primitives
Domain entities have private setters — no public property setters on Order or OrderLineItem
Entities use private constructors with static factory methods returning Result<T>
Result<T> pattern is used for operations that can fail — no throwing exceptions for business rule violations
Order aggregate enforces the $10,000 maximum total invariant
Order aggregate prevents adding items after confirmation
Order requires at least one line item (enforced at creation or confirmation)
xUnit tests exist covering all 5 business rules
Tests are behavioral — they test business outcomes, not just getters/setters
Domain events are raised for significant state changes (order created, confirmed)
